findings.title: Ambiguity in Support Agent classification cache mechanism
findings.rationale: Section 5.4.1 indicates two possible caching strategies for classify_ticket (dedicated table vs agent_run_messages), but leaves the implementation gated and pending a decision; this is critical for high-volume paths and test strategy.
findings.evidence: Section 5.4.1: "See Open Decision 11.4." and Open Decision 11.4: "dedicated cache table OR re-use agent_run_messages projection... LOC pending decision (gated)"

findings.title: Unresolved file upload path: Worker direct to S3 vs proxy
findings.rationale: Section 6.1.4 offers two physical-transit paths (worker direct to S3 or main-app proxy), with decision deferred to Open Decision 11.2; this alters IAM, error posture, and may impact reliability.
findings.evidence: Section 6.1.4: 'The two physical-transit options are gated on Open Decision 11.2' and Open Decision 11.2: 'confirm scope.'

findings.title: Incomplete contract for Support Agent prompt template ownership and update
findings.rationale: The master prompt for the Support Agent is described as 'locked, system-managed', but processes for updates and versioning are not covered; this is load-bearing for tone and compliance over time.
findings.evidence: Section 5.3.1: 'The agent is system-managed: customers cannot edit the master prompt; they can only configure per-inbox agent_config (mode, collision window, draft expiry, prompt overrides).' There is no process defined for how prompt changes are approved, versioned, or rolled back.

findings.title: Source-of-truth ambiguity between run_artifacts and iee_artifacts
findings.rationale: While section 6.1.2 distinctly calls out the separation, it does not specify what happens if an artifact exists in one but not the other (e.g., due to partial run failure); customer UI precedence and backfill are not specified for such drift.
findings.evidence: Section 6.1.2: 'Customer-facing UI reads run_artifacts only; the worker reads iee_artifacts only. No automatic backfill of pre-MVP iee_artifacts rows; only artifacts produced after the MVP ships appear in run_artifacts.'

findings.title: Eval regression set refresh policy remains open
findings.rationale: Eval regression set source and refresh is left to an open decision, but the acceptance criteria and risk register depend on its existence; lack of timeline here may block threshold enforcement.
findings.evidence: Section 5.5.2: 'Regression set | 50 to 200 historical tickets... provided by Foundry export...' and Open Decision 11.5: 'monthly refresh from Foundry; quarterly manual curation review.'

findings.title: No explicit fallback for file delivery service failure at upload time
findings.rationale: In Section 4.6.1, if S3 upload fails, the run fails and must be retried; there is no partial retry or partial-state support. This may result in artifact loss if upstream is unstable, since the system does not currently retain artifacts for later re-upload.
findings.evidence: Section 4.6.1: 'S3 upload failure ... run terminates with failureReason: artifact_upload_failed... The transcript and PDF can be regenerated by re-running the macro task; this is preferable to a half-state with a "Retry upload" affordance the schema does not currently support.'

findings.title: No defined process for prompt override injection safety
findings.rationale: Prompt overrides (such as custom voice profile) are exposed as freeform text and injected, but there is no mention of validation, sanitization, or risk-mitigation for prompt attacks or leakage.
findings.evidence: Section 5.3.2: 'promptOverride (existing): freeform textarea, used as the custom voice prompt when voiceProfile = Custom'

findings.title: Missing idempotency discussion for cross-inbox Support Agent runs
findings.rationale: While per-ticket atomic claim is well-specified, the agent run itself may touch multiple tickets/inboxes; there is no specified mechanism for crash recovery or ensuring cross-inbox events can't be double-fired if a run is partially retried.
findings.evidence: Section 5.3.4: per-ticket concurrency guard is explicit, but no higher aggregation or idempotency at the run/inbox level.

findings.title: Unspecified handling of possible prompt version drift as skills/templates are updated
findings.rationale: Skill prompt templates are called out as in need of polish, but how prompt versioning interacts with historic regression set or eval runs is not formalized -- this could produce misleading evals or unintentional drift.
findings.evidence: Section 5.4.4: '...the prompt templates for the LLM-backed skills... are stubs and need refinement per the support-desk-canonical handoff (Open Question OQ-3)...'

findings.title: No coverage of race conditions on Support Agent install and subaccount uniqueness guard
findings.rationale: Section 5.3.1 asserts singleton-per-subaccount, enforced at install time, but does not specify locking or transactionality for concurrent install attempts.
findings.evidence: Section 5.3.1: '...the install service checks for an existing active subaccount-level installation that references this system_agent_id ... and refuses if one already exists. No new schema change required for the MVP.'
